Class CsvUtils

java.lang.Object
shelter.repository.csv.CsvUtils
public final class CsvUtils extends Object
Utility class providing static helper methods for reading and writing CSV data. Handles quoting, escaping, splitting, and null-value encoding used uniformly by all CSV-backed repository implementations in this package.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static List<String>
    decodeList(String value)
    Decodes a semicolon-delimited field value back into a list of strings.

  • Method Details

    • escapeCsv

      public static String escapeCsv(String value)
      Escapes a single field value for inclusion in a CSV row. If the value is null an empty string is written; if the value contains a comma, double-quote, or newline it is wrapped in double-quotes with any embedded double-quotes doubled.
      Parameters:
      value - the field value to escape; may be null
      Returns:
      the CSV-safe representation of value

    • unescapeCsv

      public static String unescapeCsv(String value)
      Reverses the escaping applied by escapeCsv(String). An empty string is returned as null; quoted strings are unquoted and doubled double-quotes are collapsed back to single double-quotes.
      Parameters:
      value - the raw CSV token to unescape; must not be null
      Returns:
      the original string value, or null if the field was empty

    • splitCsv

      public static String[] splitCsv(String line)
      Splits a CSV row into individual field tokens, correctly handling quoted fields that may contain embedded commas or double-quotes. The number of tokens returned equals the number of comma-separated fields in line, including empty fields at either end.
      Parameters:
      line - a single CSV row; must not be null
      Returns:
      an array of raw (still-escaped) field tokens

    • encodeList

      public static String encodeList(List<String> items)
      Encodes a list of strings into a single semicolon-delimited field value for CSV storage. An empty or null list is encoded as an empty string. Individual entries that contain semicolons are not further escaped — callers must ensure entry values do not contain the delimiter character.
      Parameters:
      items - the list to encode; may be null or empty
      Returns:
      a semicolon-delimited string, or an empty string if the list is empty

    • decodeList

      public static List<String> decodeList(String value)
      Decodes a semicolon-delimited field value back into a list of strings. An empty or null input returns an empty list. Each token is trimmed of surrounding whitespace before being added to the result.
      Parameters:
      value - the encoded field value; may be null or empty
      Returns:
      a mutable list of decoded string values, never null
